summary: Russian lawmaker Svintsov claimed that American humanoid robots, likely the Phantom MK-1, have been delivered to Ukraine and are already in combat, sparking concern among Russian officials. Meanwhile, Ukrainian forces continued intensive FPV drone operations: Rubicon Center operators reported 110 enemy personnel eliminated across multiple fronts on March 18, 2026, while "Elka" anti-aircraft drones intercepted fixed-wing UAVs. Ukrainian FPV units destroyed numerous Russian drones, including Domaha, Darts, Chaklun, and others, in routine counter-UAV missions. Iranian-backed militias also launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base near Baghdad. Ukrainian forces adapted tactics, using log-periodic antennas on "Molniyas" to counter Russian jamming, and pilots employed new methods to neutralize Russian infantry, including a recorded instance of a Russian soldier charging an FPV drone with a bayonet and being struck by two simultaneous drone strikes.
